paragraph: Single-family homes in Westwood are moving fast and selling well above asking. The median price sits at $750,000 with a sale-to-list ratio of 1.09, meaning homes are fetching roughly 9 percent above list price on average. Nearly 90 percent of homes sold above asking, and the median days on market of 12 tells you sellers are dictating terms here. Seventeen sales over six months shows steady volume for a town this size. By contrast, the condo and townhouse tier is quieter with only six sales at a median of $390,500, though homes still sell slightly above list at a 1.01 ratio. That smaller volume means less room to cherry-pick, so if a condo catches your eye and fits your timeline, don't assume you have endless negotiating power. If you're comparing the two tiers in Westwood, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family home or the low-maintenance lifestyle and lower price point of a condo.
